text:
Perfect! Now when you refresh the portfolio, check the browser console. You'll see extremely detailed logging:

**What to look for in console:**

1. `🔵 Calling Zapper API for address: 0x...` - Confirms API is being called
2. `🔵 Zapper portfolio response:` - Shows the raw Zapper data
3. `🔵 Zapper token balances: X` - How many tokens returned
4. `✅ Processing Zapper tokens: X` - Starting to process
5. `Zapper total value: $X` - The total from Zapper
6. For each token: `Processing token:` with all details
7. `Creating new chain entry:` when a new chain is added
8. `Adding {TOKEN} to {CHAIN}` - Each token being added
9. `Chain {NAME} now has X tokens, value: $Y` - Running totals
10. `Zapper processing complete. Chains: X Total value: $Y`
11. `📊 Final portfolio data:` - Complete breakdown
12. `🟢 Setting portfolio data:` - What's being set to state
13. `🟢 Portfolio state updated successfully` - Confirmation

This comprehensive logging will show exactly where the issue is. If you see "No tokens found in portfolio" in the UI, it means `portfolioData.chains.length === 0`, and the console will show why.
